Mahindra Thar.mileage: close to 10 kmpl in the city and 14 kmpl on the highway (Thar 2WD)
Mahindra Thar.performance_feel: amazing as a driving experience; purely from the touch, feel and experience point of view
Mahindra Thar.torque: 300 Nm @ 1750–3000 rpm (mStallion 150 TGDi Petrol MT), 320 Nm @ 1750–3000 rpm (mStallion 150 TGDi Petrol AT), 300 Nm @ 1600–2800 rpm (mHawk 130 Diesel), 300 Nm @ 1750–2500 rpm (D117 CRDe Diesel)
Mahindra Thar.response: good and the power delivery is acceptable. While there is a hint of lag at the absolute bottom end
Mahindra Thar.city_performance: Performs exceptionally well both in city traffic and rough terrain; excellent for city cruising; easy work of city driving.
Mahindra Thar.highway_performance: Excellent for highway runs; stable at highway speeds, can cruise at 100 km/h without bother, goes past 120 km/h in a hurry, overtakes dispatched with unwavering authority.
Mahindra Thar.off_road: dispatched a reasonably challenging off-road trail, capable on steep, rocky slopes; petrol automatic 4x4 sells highest
Mahindra Thar.manual_transmission_performance: 2.2-litre diesel engine produces 152PS and 330Nm with a six-speed manual gearbox
Mahindra Thar.automatic_transmission_performance: available with a 6-speed torque converter automatic transmission; 2.2-litre diesel engine produces 175PS and 380Nm with an automatic transmission
Mahindra Thar.pedal_operation: Very light clutch operation; gear shifts click into place without effort.
Mahindra Thar.gear_shift: mechanical shift-on-the-fly capability via a dedicate 4Wd knob beside the gear shifter
Mahindra Thar.gear_selection: no vibrations that make their way in from the engine... through the pedals or the gear lever
Mahindra Thar.ride: ride quality on the road is mighty impressive. It dismisses potholes and any tarmac blemishes with contempt
Mahindra Thar.ride_quality: offers a comfortable ride (Mahindra Thar Roxx)
Mahindra Thar.stiff_on_pot_holes: dismisses potholes and any tarmac blemishes with contempt
Mahindra Thar.bumps: Stiff and bumpy at low speeds due to ladder-on-frame chassis; hard ride on rough roads; feels slightly firm, jiggly and inconsistent at slow speeds and over small bumps; becomes flatter and more absorbent over bumps, potholes, and craters when loaded or at higher speeds.
Mahindra Thar.shocks: The suspension has been changed with a multi-link setup, and independent double-wishbone front suspension with stabilizer bar and multilink rear suspension, resolving most previous weaknesses.
Mahindra Thar.nvh: NVH levels have been reduced substantially and are quite comfortable/acceptable.
Mahindra Thar.powertrain_nvh: The Thar is still noisier inside than an average SUV.
Mahindra Thar.wind_nvh: Wind noise is present, and the Thar is still noisier inside than an average SUV.
Mahindra Thar.road_nvh: The cabin can be noisy, making it not the best option for long road trips due to road noise.
Mahindra Thar.wind_noise: Wind noise is present, and the Thar is still noisier inside than an average SUV.
Mahindra Thar.tire_noise: The cabin is noisy, contributing to it not being ideal for long road trips.
Mahindra Thar.turbo_noise: The engine is noisy, yes, but the new Thar doesn't have the crudeness of the older model. Motor feels nice and refined but gets very vocal as you stretch it past the mid-range.
Mahindra Thar.jerks: Drivetrain lash like a clunk when letting go of the clutch, especially when the car is on the move.
Mahindra Thar.shakes: A bit of body-on-chassis wobble at high speeds
Mahindra Thar.shudder: There are some vibrations which can be felt lower down.
Mahindra Thar.steering: New steering wheel, similar to the XUV700
Mahindra Thar.telescopic_steering: Steering adjust rake and reach (tilt and telescopic)
Mahindra Thar.manoeuvring: Tight manoeuvring in crowded urban conditions is a bit difficult
Mahindra Thar.stability: The Thar Roxx offers good stability, making it suitable for long days on the road. It includes stability control and an electronic stability program (ESP).
Mahindra Thar.corner_stability: ride is still bouncy with noticeable body movement.
Mahindra Thar.straight_ahead_stability: pretty stable and reassuring at high speeds.
Mahindra Thar.braking: The Mahindra five-door Thar may introduce rear-wheel disc brakes.
Mahindra Thar.brakes: Front: Disc, Rear: Drum, ABS, EBD
Mahindra Thar.brake_performance: Braking 100 - 0kmph distance: 39.4 meters (for Mahindra Thar Roxx)
Mahindra Thar.vehicle_safety_features: ABS, EBD, Electronic Stability Program (ESP) with Roll-over Mitigation, Hill Hold Control, Hill Descent Control, ISOFIX Child Seat Mounts, Tyre Pressure Monitoring System (TPMS), Reverse Parking Sensors, Panic Braking Signal, Passenger Airbag Deactivation Switch
Mahindra Thar.adas: The new Mahindra Thar Roxx will include an ADAS suite.
Mahindra Thar.impact: The Mahindra Thar Roxx has achieved a 5-star safety rating in the Bharat New Car Assessment Program (BNCAP) crash tests.
Mahindra Thar.seats_restraint: The upcoming Mahindra Thar 5-door will have seat belts and seatbelt reminders for all seats, and ISOFIX child seat anchor points.
Mahindra Thar.interior: The Mahindra Thar Roxx 4x4 offers a new Mocha Brown interior option, providing a rugged and durable alternative.
Mahindra Thar.climate_control: Dual-zone climate control and rear AC vents for uniform cooling.
Mahindra Thar.seats: Multipoint recline provides comfort, though seat recline options are limited with the most comfortable position leaning towards the upright.
Mahindra Thar.seat_cushion: Up front, the seats are large and supportive
Mahindra Thar.seat_material: All-black leatherette upholstery with suede inserts.
Mahindra Thar.seat_features_detailed: driver's chair gaining height adjustment, individual centre armrests for the front passenger and driver with storage
Mahindra Thar.visibility: sitting higher up can see nicely everything around you
Mahindra Thar.soft_trims: Soft-touch materials on the upper dashboard contribute to a more premium cabin atmosphere, but the quality takes a noticeable downturn elsewhere.
Mahindra Thar.armrest: Sliding armrest and a new, wide front centre armrest.
Mahindra Thar.egress: Getting out of the rear is a bit tedious courtesy the lack of rear doors.
Mahindra Thar.ingress: Getting into the rear is a bit tedious courtesy the lack of rear doors.
Mahindra Thar.touch_response: smooth, supports Apple CarPlay and Android Auto
Mahindra Thar.drl: LED DRLs (circular design around fog lamps)
Mahindra Thar.window: Power windows front, Power windows rear
Mahindra Thar.door_effort: build quality is top-notch, giving it a solid and reliable feel.
Mahindra Thar.sensitivity: Throttle response: immediate and lunges the heavy Thar forward like a hot hatch.
Mahindra Thar.boot_space: Only adequate for a couple of small bags; continues to disappoint.
Mahindra Thar.engine_displacement: 1997 cc (mStallion 150 TGDi Petrol), 2184 cc (mHawk 130 Diesel), 1497 cc (D117 CRDe Diesel)

Hyundai Creta.performance_feel: Solid substantial feel due to suspension tuning, but can take a bit of fun away from the driving experience compared to more engaging rivals.
Hyundai Creta.torque: 144 Nm @ 4500 rpm (1.5L MPi Petrol), 253 Nm @ 1500-3500 rpm (1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol), 250 Nm @ 1500-2750 rpm (1.5L CRDi Diesel)
Hyundai Creta.response: Fast throttle response in Dynamic mode; drive modes alter throttle and gearbox responses.
Hyundai Creta.city_performance: Great for city driving; smooth ride even on poor roads.
Hyundai Creta.highway_performance: Impressive highway performance and movement on the road.
Hyundai Creta.off_road: Ground clearance is rated at 190mm; three traction control modes: snow, mud, and sand.
Hyundai Creta.manual_transmission_performance: Gear shifts are light and effortless. Shift quality is smooth, precise, and positive. The manual transmission offers quicker transitions and is forgiving even amidst imperfect executions.
Hyundai Creta.automatic_transmission_performance: 7-speed gearbox is nice and smooth; shifts are quick, not very intrusive or jerky at all, with very little head nod (DCT). The execution of IVT's artificial shifts makes it feel like it has real gears.
Hyundai Creta.pedal_operation: The pedal box has enough room for comfortable left foot rest. The clutch pedal feels quite light for a diesel, helpful in stop-and-go traffic, though one user reported it feeling a bit heavy in city traffic leading to leg fatigue. The brake pedal feel isn't the best, and the car doesn't feel surefooted under heavy braking.
Hyundai Creta.gear_shift: Gear shifts are light and effortless, precise and positive. The shift quality is smooth.
Hyundai Creta.gear_selection: Achieving that perfect gear shift is very satisfying, with the Creta being forgiving even amidst imperfect executions, implying good precision.
Hyundai Creta.stiff_on_pot_holes: It absorbs bumps without any hesitation. Even on very rough and irregular surfaces, the passengers were kept comfortable. The cabin feels well filtered from undulations on our roads.
Hyundai Creta.bumps: It absorbs bumps without any hesitation. The cabin feels well filtered from undulations on our roads.
Hyundai Creta.shocks: The new suspension set-up feels good, the new suspension tune means it handles better than ever and leans predictably.
Hyundai Creta.nvh: cabin insulation does really well; wind noise and engine noise are nicely controlled both at city speeds and on the highway.
Hyundai Creta.powertrain_nvh: engine noise is nicely controlled both at city speeds and on the highway.
Hyundai Creta.wind_nvh: Wind insulation could be a bit better though
Hyundai Creta.road_nvh: wind noise is nicely controlled both at city speeds and on the highway.
Hyundai Creta.wind_noise: Wind insulation could be a bit better though
Hyundai Creta.tire_noise: NVH levels are pretty much muted; wind noise and engine noise are nicely controlled both at city speeds and on the highway.
Hyundai Creta.jerks: A bit jerky while getting off the mark
Hyundai Creta.steering: Steering is not upto the mark. Tilt and telescopic steering adjustment along with a height adjustable driver's seat is offered as standard, which helps finding a comfortable driving position super easy.
Hyundai Creta.manoeuvring: Harder due to bigger dimensions (for Creta Grand)
Hyundai Creta.corner_stability: Body roll is well under control; one shouldn't really attack corners with the Creta.
Hyundai Creta.brakes: Front: Disc, Rear: Disc, ABS, EBD
Hyundai Creta.brake_performance: Braking performance improves dramatically with 14˝ solid discs in the rear, and 16" ventilated discs are standard up front. However, the pedal feel isn't the best, and the car doesn't feel sure-footed when applying heavy brakes, causing tires to screech in protest.
Hyundai Creta.epb: Electric Parking Brake with Auto Hold (SX(O) variant)
Hyundai Creta.airbag_types_breakdown: 6-airbag system (Driver, Passenger, Side & Curtain). Standard front-row protection includes a pair of depowered airbags, upgradeable to include thorax protection (front only) and curtain airbags that shield first and second-row occupants.
Hyundai Creta.vehicle_safety_features: ABS, EBD, ESC, Hill-start Assist Control (HAC), ISOFIX
Hyundai Creta.adas: Lane Departure Warning, Blind-Spot Collision Warning & Avoidance Assist, Forward Collision Warning & Avoidance Assist, Smart Cruise Control with Stop & Go
Hyundai Creta.impact: India-spec Hyundai Creta (Global NCAP): 3 stars for adult and child occupant protection. Scored 8/17 points for adult protection and 28.29/49 points for child protection. Adult occupant protection offers good head and knee protection to the driver, but moderate chest and poor footwell protection. Child occupant safety did not provide acceptable child safety (up to 3 years old) with excessive forward movements, but provided enough head and chest protection for the 1.5-year old. Bodyshell integrity was rated as unstable.
Hyundai Creta.seats_restraint: 3-point seatbelts and seat belt reminder for all seats
Hyundai Creta.interior: All-black interior with distinctive upholstery, N Line-spec steering wheel and gear stick
Hyundai Creta.seats: Front seats offer great comfort and support. The rear seat truly shines with its space, comfort, and convenience features, offering generous knee room, ample legroom even for six-footers, and excellent thigh support. Rear headroom is more than adequate for taller passengers. Three average-sized adults can sit comfortably in the rear seat thanks to flat cushioning, but a central headrest is missing. Seats are covered with leather upholstery and are comfortable enough for long drives. The ability to recline the seats adds an extra layer of relaxation.
Hyundai Creta.seat_cushion: nice and supportive with good side bolstering. and good support all around
Hyundai Creta.seat_material: Leather upholstery (for 2024 facelift SX(O) trim), also available in Cloth
Hyundai Creta.seat_features_detailed: Powered Front Seats, Ventilated Seats, Height Adjustable Driver Seat.
Hyundai Creta.rear_seat_features: Foldable Rear Seat, Rear Seat Center Arm Rest, Cup Holders-Rear
Hyundai Creta.visibility: Good front visibility, but rear visibility is less ideal due to rising design (based on 2018 model, but implied good overall for 2024 model)
Hyundai Creta.soft_trims: Soft-touch materials on the dashboard (implied for 2020 Creta)
Hyundai Creta.armrest: Front armrest with an integrated air purifier
Hyundai Creta.headrest: Features headrest cushions and a new third headrest (for 2025 Creta Electric)
Hyundai Creta.egress: Doors open wide enough, roof is high, no problem getting in or out.
Hyundai Creta.ingress: Doors open wide enough, roof is high, no problem getting in or out.
Hyundai Creta.seatbelt_features: 3-point seat belts for all seats with seat belt reminders. Front seatbelts have pretensioners.
Hyundai Creta.touch_response: The touchscreen audio unit is intuitive.
Hyundai Creta.apple_carplay: Apple CarPlay and Android Auto with split screen functionality.
Hyundai Creta.button: Tactile hard buttons for audio and climate controls
Hyundai Creta.audio_system: Bose Premium Sound System with 8 Speakers (SX(O) variant), 6 Speakers (S, S(O), SX variants)
Hyundai Creta.parking_camera: Rear Parking Camera with Dynamic Guidelines (all variants), Surround View Monitor (SVM) (SX(O) and above variants)
Hyundai Creta.parking_sensors: Rear Parking Sensors (all variants), Front Parking Sensors (SX(O) and above variants)
Hyundai Creta.drl: Horizon LED Positioning Lamps (S, S(O), SX, SX(O) variants), always-on
Hyundai Creta.alloy_wheel: 17-inch Diamond Cut Alloy Wheels (SX, SX(O) variants)
Hyundai Creta.tyre_size: 205/65 R16 (E, EX, S, S(O) variants), 215/60 R17 (SX, SX(O) variants)
Hyundai Creta.wiper_control: Intermittent speeds adjustable. Rain-sensing auto wipers absent (for ICE models).
Hyundai Creta.parking: Parking assist, front and rear parking sensors
Hyundai Creta.door_effort: Doors have a decent weight, not light.
Hyundai Creta.chasis: Chassis made with a high proportion of high-strength steel to keep it rigid yet lightweight
Hyundai Creta.engine_displacement: 1497 cc (1.5L MPi Petrol), 1482 cc (1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol), 1493 cc (1.5L CRDi Diesel)

Power & Torque: Hyundai Creta offers an entry-level 1.5L MPi Petrol engine with 144 Nm torque, a lower torque option not available in the Thar.
Exterior: Bi-functional LED Headlamps (SX, SX(O) variants) are available on the Creta, while the Thar uses Halogen Headlamps.
Creta offers a Panoramic Sunroof (S(O), SX, SX(O) variants), a feature absent in the Thar.
Capabilities: Creta offers a 5-seater configuration, compared to the Thar's 4-seater capacity.
Creta has a substantial boot space of 433 Litres, significantly more than Thar's 'adequate for a couple of small bags' capacity.
Creta (diesel model) achieves 0-100 kmph in 11.4 seconds, which is 13.6% faster than Thar's 13.2 seconds.
Creta offers superior mileage, with petrol variants achieving 17.4-18.4 kmpl and diesel variants 19.1-21.8 kmpl, significantly higher than Thar's ~10 kmpl city and 14 kmpl highway.
Creta has a longer wheelbase of 2610 mm, which is 160 mm (6.5%) longer than Thar's 2450 mm.
ADAS: Creta currently offers a comprehensive ADAS suite, including Lane Departure Warning, Blind-Spot Collision Warning & Avoidance Assist, Forward Collision Warning & Avoidance Assist, and Smart Cruise Control with Stop & Go. Thar mentions ADAS for a future 'Roxx' variant.
Interior: Creta features a larger 10.25-inch HD Infotainment system with Bluelink and an integrated 10.25-inch Multi-display Digital Cluster, while Thar typically features a 7-inch infotainment system.
Creta offers a premium Bose Premium Sound System with 8 Speakers (SX(O) variant), whereas Thar has roof-mounted speakers.
Creta includes front parking sensors in addition to rear parking sensors, while Thar only mentions rear parking sensors.
Creta features an Electric Parking Brake with Auto Hold (SX(O) variant), a feature not available in Thar.
Engine: Creta offers a 1482 cc (1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol) engine, which is a smaller displacement option compared to Thar's largest petrol engine.
BRAKES: Creta features rear disc brakes as standard, while Thar is equipped with rear drum brakes.
Others: Creta is equipped with 6 airbags, significantly more than Thar's 2 airbags.
Creta employs a monocoque chassis made with a high proportion of high-strength steel, designed for on-road comfort and lighter weight, contrasting with Thar's rugged ladder frame.
Creta's price range extends higher to ₹19.99 Lakh (₹3.05 Lakh higher than Thar's top of ₹16.94 Lakh) and starts lower at ₹10.99 Lakh (₹0.36 Lakh lower than Thar's start of ₹11.35 Lakh).
Creta achieves significantly higher monthly sales (above 18,500 units/month) compared to Thar (3,500-4,000 units/month).

Power & Torque: Thar offers higher peak torque for its petrol engines (up to 320 Nm for mStallion 150 TGDi Petrol AT, 26.5% higher than Creta's 1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol 253 Nm).
Thar's diesel engines also offer higher peak torque (300 Nm, 20% higher than Creta's 1.5L CRDi Diesel 250 Nm).
Engine: Thar offers larger engine displacement options, including a 1997 cc petrol engine and a 2184 cc diesel engine, compared to Creta's largest engines (1497 cc petrol, 1493 cc diesel).
Others: Thar utilizes a rugged ladder frame underpinning chassis, specifically designed for heavy-duty off-road use and structural rigidity.
Thar is equipped with a low-ratio 4WD mode that can multiply torque 2.48 times, explicitly engineered for challenging off-road trails and steep, rocky slopes.
Thar boasts significantly higher ground clearance of 226 mm, which is 36 mm (18.9%) more than Creta's 190 mm, providing better obstacle clearance.
Thar achieved a higher 4-star Global NCAP safety rating, compared to Creta's 3-star Global NCAP rating.
